present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly gives something to someone every single day.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly gives advice that nobody asked for.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly gave her neighbour some flowers from the garden.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly gave the wrong present to the wrong person.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will give everyone a lovely present this Christmas.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will give up wrapping and use a bag.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has given a gift to everyone in the family.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has given away the good umbrella. He does not know to whom.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly keeps every card and letter the grandchildren send her.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly keeps every newspaper since 1986.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly kept every promise she ever made to her family.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly kept the wrong receipts and threw the right ones away.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will keep smiling every day of her life.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will keep sitting in his chair. That is his plan.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has kept this family together through everything.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has kept all his newspapers. The garage is full.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly shares her food, her time and her kindness.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly shares things when there is nothing left to keep.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly shared her last biscuit with the postman.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly shared a story about himself for forty-five minutes.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will share everything she has with her neighbours.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will share his opinion on everything, wanted or not.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has shared her home, her table and her heart.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has shared his opinion six times today already.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly smiles when she wakes up every morning.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly smiles when he sees his favourite biscuits.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly smiled at every person she passed on her walk.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly smiled when he found the TV remote under the sofa.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will smile at everyone she sees today.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will smile when dinner is ready.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has smiled all day and made everyone feel happy.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has smiled twice today. Once at food and once at the TV.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly always says please and thank you.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly says sorry a lot but keeps doing the same things.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly said something kind to everyone she met.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly said the wrong name to the right person.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will say goodbye and leave a little gift.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will say he will do it and then not do it.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has said something kind to everyone today.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has said sorry to three people today.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly knows every person in the village by name.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly knows what he likes and not much else.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly knew exactly what to say to cheer her friend up.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly knew the answer but said the wrong thing anyway.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will know what to do in any situation.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will not know where he put his glasses.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has known her best friend for forty-five years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has known his neighbour for twenty years and still gets his name wrong.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly feels grateful for every single day.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly feels cold, tired or hungry. Usually all three.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly felt very happy at the village party.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly felt confused all morning and some of the afternoon.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will feel better after her walk and a cup of tea.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will feel better after a nap. He always does.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has felt happy all week and plans to continue.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has felt tired all week. It is the chair.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly loves her family, her garden and her neighbours.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly loves his TV, his chair and his biscuits.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly loved every moment of the village fair.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly loved the food at the fair and ate too much of it.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will love every day she has with her family.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will love Mrs Bubbly every day for the rest of his life.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has loved this family with all her heart.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has loved Mrs Bubbly for fifty years. He would do it all again.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly carries on and keeps going no matter what.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly carries on doing things the same way and expects different results.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly carried on cheerfully after the roof started leaking.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly carried on talking through the film and missed the whole story.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will carry on being wonderful, as always.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will carry on as normal. Everyone expects it now.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has carried on through every difficult moment.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has carried on doing things his own way. The results are interesting.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly looks forward to every new day with a smile.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly looks forward to lunch, dinner and sitting in his chair.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly looked forward to the village party for two weeks.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly looked forward to the party but fell asleep during the first song.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will look forward to seeing her family next week.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will look forward to the weekend because of the football.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has always looked forward to Mondays.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has looked forward to his nap all morning.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly gets on with everyone she has ever met.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly gets on with things in his own time, at his own pace.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly got on with the new neighbours straight away.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly got on with fixing the shelf and broke two more.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will get on with the baking this afternoon.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will get on with the garage eventually.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has always got on with everyone around her.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has got on with his day at his own speed.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly lives every day as if it is a gift.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly lives a comfortable life in his favourite chair.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly lived in France for a year when she was young.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly lived next to the sea for two years and never once went for a swim.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will live every day with joy and purpose.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will live happily and comfortably for a very long time.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has lived a full, happy and wonderful life.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has lived a very comfortable life and is grateful for every biscuit.
